item.title: F*** it, good enough! Down 65 lbs and feel good... not perfect by any stretch but i think this is about right
item.description:
15 short of my original goal, but feel like this is a reasonable place to land. Still got the pixar butt, that's mine forever
215. Never again. Everything hurt all the time and I hid from cameras for years.
item.dc:creator: RufusTheThirteenthApostle

item.title: Chambers
item.description: Just to be clear, this isn’t Old English like Beowulf—it’s modern English written in an old-fashioned or archaic style, more like Elizabethan or Victorian English. TLNR go read Beowulf

https://pll.harvard.edu/course/citizen-politics-america-public-opinion-elections-interest-groups-and-media and https://pll.harvard.edu/course/us-political-institutions-congress-presidency-courts-and-bureaucracy
https://pll.harvard.edu/course/leadership-creating-public-value and https://pll.harvard.edu/course/justice

item.title: sci-fi shenanigans & epic geekery (part 464)
item.dc:creator: lydecker17

item.title: I'm turning my students into rocket engineers!
item.description:
https://imgur.com/gallery/help-science-teacher-make-lifetime-memories-teach-lifetime-lessons-2LjXIAz That meme dump posted about 36 hours ago raised $3,200 to buy model rockets for my students at Howard Middle School. Y'all are AMAZING. Pat yourselves on the back. If you want to donate more in the form of gift cards, I'll buy larger rockets to launch for the kids, as well as try to get enough engines that they can all launch twice again like they have the last two years. https://www.donorschoose.org/project/rocketry-for-all/9308644/?rf=link-dc-2025-05-teacher_profile-teacher_8939248&challengeid=22095163&utm_source=dc&utm_medium=page&utm_campaign=project&utm_term=teacher_8939248 I love you imgur! Here is what you can expect: A post from me sometime in August after the rockets come in. It will be here on Imgur. Throughout the 2025-2026 school year, I will make posts here to keep you advised about how our year is going. in March/April of 2026 when we get to our unit on space, my kids will build and launch their own rockets. We are going to punch holes in the sky!!!!!
March 1970. There is an episode of Star Trek my mother had not seen before on TV. She is also nine months pregnant. Shortly after the episode starts, she goes into labor. But mom LOVES Star Trek, so she toughs it out until it is over. In the hospital after I am born, she wants to name me "Deforest"; after the actor who plays Leonard "Bones" McCoy. Thankfully Dad shut that down, so Mom picked James instead. I also did not get Tiberius as my middle name. I do love Star Trek, but I love Star Wars more. By Grabthar's Hammer. THANK YOU IMGUR!!!!!
This is me. Note the lightsaber. It is purple. Why? Ask Samuel Jackson. Yes, I wear kilts to school. The kids love it. I am the Jedi Teacher. I love you all so much, and my kids next year have no idea what is about to hit them. Thank you, thank you, thank you. Follow me to see my posts about teaching here and for updates on our rocket building in the spring of next year.
item.dc:creator: JediTeacher

item.title: The city of Istanbul is no stranger to historical architecture. This building is a remarkable example of how historical cities are layered over time, each level is a chapter in a story that stretches back through antiquity
item.description: The city of Istanbul is no stranger to historical architecture. This building is a remarkable example of how historical cities are layered over time, each level is a chapter in a story that stretches back through antiquity and spans multiple historical time periods.� The structure’s ground floor dates back more than 1,800 years and has been built upon and adapted repeatedly over the centuries.� Beyond being a fascinating piece of architecture in a city steeped in history, this building also stands as a testament to the craftsmanship of past builders, a record of their skill and hard work. It’s a powerful example of historical architecture that tells a story and is a reminder of how the past and present connect.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: I lied, a meme dump
item.description:
Root beer, humans love it.
Something, something poyo...
*undoes giant novelty Texas belt bucle*
In this economy?
More fun than it sounds like, please wear wading shoes though or you'll crack your head slipping on rock grime.
Soundscloud is nice, but please don't forget to press some actual physical copies of that EP.
Fast and more fun than waiting for the government to get back to you about money they owe vs. money you owe.
Save room, we're having Akira for dessert.
We're making our own series marathon, with blackjack and hookers.
*loud fan SFX*
It's kind of funny, but don't actually do this. Whippets will make literal holes in your brain - not kidding.
Which one though? Original series, the Animated series, Next Generation, Deep Space Nine, Voyager, Enterprise, Discovery, Picard, Strange New Wolrds, or Lower Decks? So much Trek, so little time.
"Agent 408 requests a shipment of surveillance equipment and cheeseburgers with which to honeypot Krasnov?"
*Hat Man intensifies*
This next one is going to blow your socks clean off though.
item.dc:creator: circlebreaker

.
.

item.title: Have some mildly interresting stuff
item.description:
do not to confuse with the poopknife.
https://www.youtube.com/@ActionLabShorts/shorts
https://www.youtube.com/shorts/f56ppr0rq1k 1800's pencil sharpener
https://www.youtube.com/shorts/ujJ58kSz8Iw 1890's milkshaker. I could teach you how to do this, but i'll have to charge you because it's better than yours.
Pantone 7488 As always, if you know any sources, share them and I'll gladly add them.
